Acta v0.2.0 record · Generated from accepted canonical state (decisions/direction-decision-slug.md) after explicit acceptance (chat — explicit confirmation of the pasted candidate) · Never generated from browser state.

decision record · ACCEPTED

Accepted interface direction

One paragraph stating which direction was accepted and the decisive reason.

Accepted direction

Decision direction-decision-slug

First direction — One sentence stating this direction's philosophy about the surface.

Rationale: The accepted rationale, in the human's words.. Accepted via chat — explicit confirmation.

Question decided: Which visual direction should this surface take?

Comparison evidence

Same dimensions, all directions, as compared before acceptance
DimensionFirst directionSecond directionThird direction
A dimension every direction is judged onYes — evidencePartly — evidenceNo — evidence

Rejected directions — preserved

Second direction

A genuinely different philosophy — not a restyle of direction A.

Would win when:

  • a real strength observed in the render

Cost that lost the comparison:

  • a real cost observed in the render

Third direction

A third incompatible philosophy so reacting beats imagining.

Would win when:

  • a real strength observed in the render

Cost that lost the comparison:

  • a real cost observed in the render

Unresolved at acceptance

  • anything the decision deliberately leaves open

Sources

  1. [E1] the current markup or screens the directions must replace

Acta is a print-first engineering record. Fact = directly supported; Inference = reasoned from evidence; Simplification = deliberately reduced model. This record renders accepted canonical state only; open decisions live in the companion instrument.
